description_long: This awning is attached to the side of a two-story building, extending outward to provide shelter over a ground-level area. The canopy is a muted brown color with a subtle, slightly textured appearance, and it is supported by a fixed frame structure that connects to the building facade. The awning's roof is pitched, allowing rainwater to run off, and its edge trim is straight and unadorned, giving it a functional, utilitarian look. The main building features a dark, gabled roof and multiple windows, while the awning itself is clearly a distinct architectural addition. No branding, signage, or decorative elements are visible on the canopy or its trim. The overall design suggests durability and practicality, suitable for residential or small commercial urban environments.
description: A brown, pitched-roof awning with a straight edge trim, fixed to the side of a two-story building, supported by a sturdy frame and lacking any visible signage or decoration.
description_view_0: Front view showing the awning extending from the building, with the pitched canopy and supporting frame clearly visible.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the full length of the awning and its attachment to the building, with the straight edge trim and roof slope apparent.
description_view_2: Rear angled view showing the awning's integration with the building and the supporting structure beneath the canopy.
description_view_3: Back view of the building, with the awning mostly obscured and only the roof and part of the frame visible.
attribute_mounting_location: attached to the side facade of a two-story building, above ground level
attribute_support_structure_type: fixed frame attached to building facade
